trip_title: Mexico City (Roma Norte/Condesa) boutique stay + local food/arts itinerary

hotels.name: Casa Mali by Dominion
hotels.description: Chic accommodations with kitchenettes in a polished hotel featuring breakfast & a rooftop patio.

hotels.name: Hotel Villa Condesa
hotels.neighbourhood: Roma Nte., Cuauhtémoc, Ciudad de México
hotels.description: Unfussy rooms in an upscale hotel offering a garden, a restaurant & a lounge with a fireplace.

hotels.name: La Valise mx City, an SLH Hotel
hotels.neighbourhood: Roma Nte., Cuauhtémoc, Ciudad de México
hotels.description: Trendy, individually furnished suites in a polished property featuring complimentary Wi-Fi.

hotels.name: Casa Decu
hotels.neighbourhood: Hipódromo Condesa, Cuauhtémoc, Ciudad de México
hotels.description: Stylish rooms & suites in a hip hotel featuring a rooftop garden & a gym, plus complimentary Wi-Fi.

weather.packing_tip: Layers: warm jacket/sweater in the morning + lighter layer for afternoons.

activities.name: Check-in + neighborhood drift (Roma Norte → Condesa)
activities.address: Roma Norte / Hipódromo Condesa, Cuauhtémoc, Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Drop bags, then do a slow, no-agenda walk to get the rhythm of your block and nearby parks.

activities.name: Mercado Medellín
activities.address: Mercado Medellín #20, Campeche 101, Roma Sur, Cuauhtémoc, 06760 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Indoor produce market with stalls—great for an easy first “local food” lunch (tacos/soups/fruit).

transit.instructions: Head to Mercado Medellín for a relaxed market lunch; don’t overthink it—order what looks busy at the counter.

activities.name: Jazzatlán Capital
activities.address: Guanajuato 239, Roma Nte., Cuauhtémoc, 06700 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Bohemian wine-sharing vibe with live music—good for an “end the night like locals” moment.

transit.instructions: If it’s not too cold for you, walk partway back; otherwise take a quick ride to save energy.

weather.packing_tip: Coffee- and park-friendly layers: beanie + light jacket for mornings; t-shirt under a sweater for afternoons.

activities.name: Medellín Market (leftovers / breakfast bites)
activities.address: Mercado Medellín #20, Campeche 101, Roma Sur, Cuauhtémoc, 06760 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Start with quick fruit/juice + one warm bite (taco/soup) from whoever is busiest at the stall.

transit.instructions: Go early-ish so you can eat fast and still have a long walking afternoon.

activities.name: Mercado de Artesanías La Ciudadela
activities.address: Balderas S/N, Colonia Centro, Centro, Cuauhtémoc, 06040 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Pick up traditional crafts/folk art—best when you wander without a strict shopping list.

transit.instructions: Ride into Centro for a slower artisan browsing session; come back before it gets too dark.

activities.name: Casa Franca
activities.address: Mérida 109, Roma Nte., Cuauhtémoc, 06700 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Warm, laid-back watering hole; great if you want a grown-up drink with live jazz energy.

transit.instructions: Head back to Roma for a jazz-forward dinner/drink; keep it simple on a weeknight.

weather.packing_tip: Sunscreen + layers—sun in the afternoon feels warm, but evenings cool quickly.

activities.name: Florería / Mercado Jamaica
activities.address: Guillermo Prieto 45, Jamaica, Venustiano Carranza, 15800 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Go early: flowers in every direction + quick grab-and-go food. Great for a chaotic-but-fun local start.

transit.instructions: Arrive hungry; eat one market snack early, then browse without committing to souvenirs.

activities.name: Laguna (PRODUCTORA)
activities.address: Doctor Lucio 181, Doctores, Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Former factory turned creative complex—good for wandering studios, small exhibitions, and courtyard coffee.

transit.instructions: Head to Doctores for a creative afternoon—keep it casual and flexible.

activities.name: Bajo Circuito Multiforo Urbano
activities.address: Bajo Puente de, Cto. Interior S/N, Colonia Condesa, Cuauhtémoc, 06140 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: If there’s a show/workshop on your dates, this is a legit place for DIY energy and local crowds.

transit.instructions: Check what’s on—if there’s a live set, plan to stay; if not, use it as a stop for the vibe and move on.

weather.packing_tip: Even though days feel pleasant, evenings can be chilly—pack a sweater you’ll actually wear.

activities.name: Mercado De San Cosme
activities.address: C. Gabino Barreda 18, San Rafael, Cuauhtémoc, 06470 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Local market with produce/meat stalls and casual prepared options—great for a slower, neighborhood breakfast.

transit.instructions: Eat lightly at first; save appetite for the afternoon wandering and dinner.

activities.name: Casa Comtesse (coffee/slow lounge stop)
activities.address: Av. Benjamín Franklin 197, Hipódromo, Cuauhtémoc, 06100 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: A convenient nearby stop to reset—order a warm drink and people-watch from the lounge vibe.

transit.instructions: If it’s too chilly for a long walk, take a quick taxi/ride; otherwise use it to explore San Rafael/Hipódromo edges.

activities.name: Jazzatlán Capital (second round if you loved it)
activities.address: Guanajuato 239, Roma Nte., Cuauhtémoc, 06700 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: If you want consistency: grab a sharing plate and let the music lead.

transit.instructions: Keep dinner casual—this place works well as your full evening plan.

weather.packing_tip: Bring a medium jacket + comfy shoes—January mornings can feel colder than you expect at altitude.

activities.name: Coyoacán Market (if you want a different vibe)
activities.address: Ignacio Allende s/n, Del Carmen, Coyoacán, 04100 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Neighborhood marketplace with food + crafts. Great for a “less Roma/Condesa” morning.

transit.instructions: Use this as your change-of-scene day: wander first, then eat whatever smells best.

activities.name: Mercado Sonora
activities.address: Fray Servando Teresa de Mier 419, Merced Balbuena, Venustiano Carranza, 15810 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Fun, old-school market energy: food, small gifts, and lots of herbs/curios (daytime only).

transit.instructions: Aim for daylight browsing; pick one snack and one small souvenir max.

activities.name: Departamento (terrace bar + live music vibe)
activities.address: Av. Álvaro Obregón 154-primer piso, Roma Nte., Cuauhtémoc, 06700 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Laid-back terrace spot—good for a late dinner + drinks without club pressure.

transit.instructions: Settle back in Roma for your last “easy but fun” night.

weather.packing_tip: Last-day checklist: comfy layers + a backup warm layer for taxi-to-airport chills.

activities.name: La Merced Market (organized chaos)
activities.address: Circunvalación, La Merced, Zona Centro, Venustiano Carranza, 15100 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Big traditional market—grab one warm thing for breakfast/brunch and browse for produce/cheese/spices.

transit.instructions: Go earlier for fewer crowds; keep valuables close in the busiest aisles.

activities.name: Pack + café time near your hotel (Roma/Condesa)
activities.address: Roma Norte / Condesa, Cuauhtémoc, Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: Use this as your flexible slot: buy snacks for the airport, do laundry/convenience store runs, and squeeze one last coffee before heading out.

transit.instructions: Short ride back to your area for a calm, not-stressed finish.

activities.name: Departure night plan: keep it simple
activities.address: Roma Norte / Condesa, Cuauhtémoc, Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico
activities.description: If you’re flying later: order something quick for dinner nearby, then call it. If you’re departing earlier: skip this block.

budget_summary: Mentioned target is a boutique hotel in Roma Norte/Condesa under ~$150/night; no other explicit trip budget amounts were provided.

safety.advisory_summary: Mexico City travel: follow your home country’s travel advisory. Use extra caution around busy areas, avoid traveling alone at night, and take care with belongings and transportation. Some market areas (e.g., Merced/central markets, Tepito) are described in sources as higher-risk and should be visited in daylight with situational awareness.
safety.visa_requirements: Verify visa requirements for your nationality with your government/embassy (not specified in the provided text).
health_requirements: No specific vaccination requirements stated in the provided text.
General guidance: ensure routine vaccinations are up to date; consider country-specific recommendations from your health authority (not provided in the text).
seasonal_risks: January is cool mornings and mild/warm afternoons; pack layers.
Dry season timing is referenced (Oct–Apr) with generally low rain; however temperature swings can be significant.
natural_hazards: Mexico City is in a seismically active region (earthquakes possible).
safety_tips: Check your government’s travel advisory for Mexico before traveling.
Use daylight for higher-activity/market areas described as less safe; don’t wander after dark in those zones.
Keep valuables secure and be cautious with pickpocketing in crowded places.
Use registered taxis/rideshare and avoid isolated routes late at night.
Have emergency contacts and know how to reach local services quickly.

etiquette_tips: Dress in comfortable clothing and use layers (cool mornings, warmer afternoons).
Book Rosca de Reyes slices/Three Kings festivities/seasonal events around Jan 6; expect stores/markets to be busy near early January.
When visiting markets or nightlife spots, avoid flashy/expensive jewelry; blend in with locals.
Use daylight for higher-intensity markets/areas (e.g., La Merced/Tepito-style experiences) and avoid staying out late without local context.
culture.tipping_guide: Mexico City: typical service-tip guidance (if not already included) is ~10% of the bill for restaurant/table service; for taxis and small services round up or tip a few pesos. For bartenders/cafés, a few pesos per drink is common—adjust upward for excellent service or if you’re in a higher-end venue.
dining_customs: Go to mercados for an on-the-ground meal (locals often eat quickly and with purpose).
Order at the stall/counter and take items to your table; ask what’s fresh if you’re unsure.
Start with something simple at Mercado Medellín/Jamaica rather than over-planning where to eat first.
Street food and micheladas are a big part of local market culture; bring small cash for quick purchases.
religious_customs: Día de los Reyes / Three Kings Day (Jan 6): Rosca de Reyes is served; a tiny doll is hidden in the bread—whoever finds it in their slice traditionally has follow-up duties (parties/hosting) later in the season.
dress_code_notes: Layer for January: cool mornings/ evenings and mild/warm afternoons.
Comfortable shoes are important for uneven sidewalks and market walking.
Avoid overly flashy outfits/jewelry in crowded markets if you want to blend in.
food_specialties: Mercado Medellín: hearty local eats for day-one market lunch.
La Merced: intense big-market street snacks (go with a plan).
Rosca de Reyes (Three Kings bread) around Jan 6.
music_and_arts: Roma/Condesa nightlife has a strong electronic + indie/art-crowd vibe; look for venues like Bar Oriente, Fünk Club, Foro Indie Rocks!, Circo Volador.
Doctores: Laguna is an independent, former-factory creative space with exhibitions and public cultural programming.

packing.reason: January mornings/evenings in Mexico City can feel chilly compared with daytime; a real layer keeps you comfortable for walks after dark.
packing.weather_context: January typically brings cool mornings and mild/warm afternoons at Mexico City’s altitude.
packing.activity_context: Evening strolls around Roma/Condesa and nights out.
